You are a flag detection model for government RFP screening. Your job is to identify which flags, if any, are present in the provided RFP text chunk.

You will be given:
- A list of relevant flag definitions and examples (retrieved context)
- An RFP text chunk to analyze

Rules:
- Output one flag name per line, using only the exact flag names listed below
- If no flags are present, output: no_flag
- Do not output explanations, reasoning, or any other text
- Only detect a flag when the evidence in the chunk is explicit — do not infer from weak signals

Valid flags:
waterfall_methodology, off_the_shelf_software, no_custom_development, lpta_source_selection,
small_business_set_aside, 8a_set_aside, wosb_set_aside, edwosb_set_aside, sdvosb_set_aside,
hubzone_set_aside, agile_methodology, oral_presentation, design_exercise, budget_too_low,
brownfield, onsite_required, onsite_madison, large_team, marginal_short_duration, no_flag

[Retrieved context]

Example 1:
The Social Security Administration is seeking a vendor to implement a COTS document management and workflow automation solution within the Office of Disability Determinations. This is a firm-fixed-price acquisition for commercial software licensing, implementation, and ongoing support. The scope of this contract is expressly limited to the deployment and configuration of an existing commercial product. No custom software development will be permitted. Offerors may not propose to write new code, develop new application interfaces, or engineer bespoke processing logic to satisfy SSA-specific business rules. Where the commercial product includes configurable rules engines, form builders, or workflow designers, the Contractor may use those tools to tailor the solution to SSA requirements. Such activities constitute configuration and are within scope. However, any effort requiring software compilation, version branching, or source code modification is strictly prohibited and shall not be included in any proposal. SSA reserves the right to reject any proposal that conflates configuration services with software development activity.

Example 2:
The Department of Veterans Affairs is seeking a Commercial Off-the-Shelf (COTS) solution for enterprise human resources management. Offerors must provide a fully licensed, commercially available software platform that meets the functional requirements outlined in this solicitation. No custom software development will be performed under this contract. The Government requires configuration only to align the solution with VA-specific workflows, user roles, and reporting hierarchies. Vendors who propose proprietary or purpose-built applications developed specifically for this requirement will be considered non-responsive. The selected solution must be a product currently available in the commercial marketplace with an established user base of no fewer than fifty enterprise customers. Implementation services shall be limited to system configuration, data migration, and user training. All modifications to core application functionality are expressly prohibited. The Contractor shall document all configuration settings to ensure repeatability and auditability. Any identified gaps between COTS functionality and agency requirements shall be addressed through process re-engineering on the Government side, not through development of new software features.

Example 3:
The Department of Homeland Security, Office of the Chief Information Officer, hereby solicits proposals for an enterprise identity and access management solution. This requirement is for a COTS solution required to meet the agency's zero-trust architecture objectives. The Government will not fund, authorize, or accept delivery of any custom-developed software components. Offerors are advised that the scope of this engagement is configuration only, encompassing identity provider setup, multi-factor authentication policy definition, role-based access control mapping, and directory synchronization. All proposed software must be commercially available and actively maintained by the Offeror as a standard product offering. Licensing terms must reflect commercial market rates, and the product must receive regular security patches and version updates as part of the vendor's standard release cycle. Integration connectors used to link the platform with DHS legacy systems must be pre-built and commercially supported. Engineering of new connectors or middleware specifically for this contract is outside the permissible scope of work.

---

RFP chunk to analyze:
The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, Office of Acquisition and Grants Management, is issuing this solicitation for a vendor management and contract lifecycle management platform to centralize procurement oversight functions across the agency. This procurement is limited in scope to the acquisition and implementation of an existing commercial software product. No custom development is permitted under this contract. CMS explicitly prohibits the design, coding, testing, or deployment of software functionality that does not exist in the Offeror's commercial product at the time of proposal submission. The Government's intent is to standardize its contract management operations around a proven platform and to re-engineer internal workflows as necessary to align with the tool's native capabilities. Permissible implementation activities are confined to system configuration, including contract template creation, approval routing setup, obligation tracking parameter definition, and role-based permission assignments. Integration with CMS financial systems shall be accomplished exclusively through pre-built, vendor-supported connectors or standard API frameworks already available in the commercial product. Offerors who propose to develop new integration middleware, build custom reporting engines, or engineer enhancements to the platform's core modules in response to CMS-specific requirements will be found non-responsive to this solicitation. All configuration decisions shall be documented in a Configuration Workbook deliverable submitted prior to system go-live, enabling the Government to recreate the configured environment independently if required.
no_custom_development

Example 1:
DEPARTMENT OF VETERANS AFFAIRS
Veterans Health Administration
Solicitation No. VA-258-24-R-0441

TYPE OF SET-ASIDE: HUBZone Set-Aside

Pursuant to the authority granted under FAR Subpart 19.13, this requirement has been designated as a HUBZone small business set-aside. The Government anticipates awarding a firm-fixed-price contract for medical transcription services to a concern that qualifies as a HUBZone small business. In order to be eligible for award, the offeror must be listed in the System for Award Management (SAM) as a certified HUBZone small business and must hold a valid HUBZone certification issued by the U.S. Small Business Administration at the time of submission. The contracting officer has determined that there is a reasonable expectation that at least two or more HUBZone small business concerns will submit offers at a fair and reasonable price. Accordingly, competition is being restricted exclusively to HUBZone-certified firms. Offerors are reminded that misrepresentation of HUBZone status is a violation of federal law and may result in debarment, fines, and criminal prosecution. The period of performance is twelve months with two option years.

Example 2:
SOCIAL SECURITY ADMINISTRATION
Office of Acquisition and Grants
Solicitation: SSA-RFP-24-1147
Program: Administrative Records Management Services

SET-ASIDE DESIGNATION: HUBZone Small Business

The Social Security Administration hereby issues this Request for Proposals as a total set-aside for HUBZone small business concerns under the authority of FAR 19.1305 and the Small Business Act. Only firms with active and valid HUBZone certifications granted by the Small Business Administration are eligible to submit offers in response to this solicitation. Offers received from firms not certified as HUBZone small businesses will be rejected as technically unacceptable without evaluation of price or technical merit. The applicable NAICS code is 518210, Cloud Computing and Data Processing Services, with a size standard of $47.0 million. Offerors must provide their SBA HUBZone certification letter dated within the past twelve months or demonstrate active status in the SBA database. The requirement covers a base year of twelve months with options for four additional twelve-month periods. HUBZone businesses interested in this procurement are encouraged to register on beta.SAM.gov and ensure their HUBZone designation is current. The total estimated value including all option periods is approximately $8.7 million.

Example 3:
U.S. ARMY CORPS OF ENGINEERS
Huntsville Center
Solicitation Reference: W912DY-24-R-0057
Subject: Environmental Remediation Services

SMALL BUSINESS PROGRAM: HUBZone Set-Aside

This procurement is conducted as a competitive HUBZone small business set-aside pursuant to 15 U.S.C. § 657a and FAR 19.1305(a). The Contracting Officer has determined that two or more HUBZone small business concerns are likely to submit competitive offers for these environmental remediation services. Therefore, all competition under this solicitation is restricted to firms holding a current, valid HUBZone certification from the Small Business Administration. Offerors must represent and certify their HUBZone status in the Representations and Certifications section of SAM.gov. The applicable NAICS code is 562910 (Remediation Services) with a size standard of $22.0 million. The Government reserves the right to cancel this HUBZone set-aside and reissue under a different set-aside category or as an unrestricted procurement if an insufficient number of qualified HUBZone small business offers are received. Firms are encouraged to verify their HUBZone certification is current before investing resources in proposal development. Performance location is Fort Campbell, Kentucky.

---

RFP chunk to analyze:
DEPARTMENT OF THE INTERIOR
Bureau of Land Management
Office of Acquisition and Property Management
Request for Proposals
Solicitation No.: BLM-NV-2024-R-0072

SCOPE AND SET-ASIDE STATUS

The Bureau of Land Management, Nevada State Office, intends to award a firm-fixed-price contract for invasive species removal and habitat restoration services across designated public lands in the Great Basin region. The estimated period of performance is a base year of twelve months with four one-year option periods.

Pursuant to the authority set forth in the Small Business Act, 15 U.S.C. 657a, and implemented through FAR 19.1305, this acquisition has been set aside exclusively for Historically Underutilized Business Zone (HUBZone) small business concerns. The contracting officer has made a reasonable determination that two or more HUBZone small business concerns will submit competitive offers at fair and reasonable prices. Accordingly, all competition under this solicitation is restricted to certified HUBZone small business firms.

To be eligible for award, offerors must demonstrate active HUBZone certification through the SBA at the time of offer submission. The Government reserves the right to verify each offeror's HUBZone status directly through the SBA's online HUBZone database prior to making any award determination. Offers received from firms that are not certified HUBZone small businesses at the time of submission will be deemed nonresponsive and eliminated from consideration without further evaluation.

The NAICS code applicable to this requirement is 561730 (Landscaping Services), with an associated small business size standard of $9.0 million. Contractors are reminded that maintaining the principal office and the required percentage of HUBZone-resident employees is a continuing obligation throughout contract performance, consistent with 13 C.F.R. Part 126.

Example 1:
DEPARTMENT OF VETERANS AFFAIRS
VA REGIONAL CONTRACTING OFFICE – SOUTHEAST
Solicitation No.: VA-247-24-R-0118

SET-ASIDE NOTICE: This solicitation is issued as an Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business (EDWOSB) set-aside in accordance with FAR 19.1505(b). The Department of Veterans Affairs seeks qualified vendors to provide comprehensive medical transcription and clinical documentation improvement services at designated VA Medical Centers throughout the southeastern region. This requirement falls under NAICS 561410, Telephone Answering Services, with a size standard of $19 million. To be eligible for award, an offeror must represent itself as an Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business concern and must be certified as an EDWOSB by the Small Business Administration or an SBA-approved third-party certifier. Certification must be active and valid at the time of offer submission and remain valid through contract award. The contracting officer has determined that this requirement is appropriate for an EDWOSB set-aside based on market research indicating a reasonable expectation that at least two EDWOSB concerns will submit offers at fair market prices. Prospective offerors that do not qualify under the EDWOSB program will not be considered for this award.

Example 2:
DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION
FEDERAL HIGHWAY ADMINISTRATION
Solicitation Number: FHWA-2024-SS-00091

NOTICE OF SET-ASIDE: The Federal Highway Administration (FHWA), in accordance with the Women-Owned Small Business Federal Contract Program established under 15 U.S.C. 637(m) and implemented at FAR Subpart 19.15, hereby designates this procurement as a total set-aside for Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business (EDWOSB) concerns. The FHWA requires professional environmental consulting services to support National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) compliance reviews for proposed highway infrastructure projects in the Mountain West region. The applicable NAICS code is 541620 – Environmental Consulting Services, with a size standard of $19 million in average annual receipts. The contracting officer's market research has determined that there is a reasonable expectation that two or more EDWOSB concerns capable of performing the required work at a fair market price will submit offers. Eligible offerors must be certified as Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Businesses through the SBA's EDWOSB certification process. Firms that are certified only as Women-Owned Small Businesses (WOSB) but not as Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Businesses are not eligible for this set-aside. All EDWOSB certifications must be current and reflected in SAM.gov at time of offer.

Example 3:
DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE
AGRICULTURAL RESEARCH SERVICE
Solicitation Number: 12-25-B-0034

This solicitation is being conducted as an Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business (EDWOSB) set-aside in accordance with FAR Subpart 19.15 and 13 CFR Part 127. The USDA Agricultural Research Service (ARS) requires logistics, warehousing, and distribution services in support of research specimen and laboratory supply chain operations at the National Centers for Animal Health complex in Ames, Iowa. The applicable NAICS code for this requirement is 493110 – General Warehousing and Storage Facilities, and the applicable size standard is $34 million in average annual receipts. Pursuant to the EDWOSB set-aside designation, only offers submitted by firms that are currently certified as Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business concerns will be considered. To be considered economically disadvantaged, the woman or women who own and control the firm must meet the personal financial criteria established at 13 CFR 127.200, including limitations on personal net worth, adjusted gross income, and total assets. Offerors should include their SBA-issued EDWOSB certification number or third-party certifier documentation in Volume I of their offer. Awards made under this solicitation are subject to the limitations on subcontracting requirements applicable to EDWOSB set-asides as specified in FAR 52.219-30.

---

RFP chunk to analyze:
NATIONAL AERONAUTICS AND SPACE ADMINISTRATION
GLENN RESEARCH CENTER
Solicitation No.: NNC24ZA002R

This combined synopsis/solicitation is issued in accordance with FAR Part 12 and FAR Part 13, as applicable, and constitutes the only solicitation for this requirement. Offers are being solicited, and a written solicitation document will not be issued separately. This procurement is conducted as an Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business (EDWOSB) set-aside pursuant to FAR 19.1505(b) and the Women-Owned Small Business Federal Contract Program regulations found at 13 CFR Part 127. NASA Glenn Research Center requires technical writing, documentation development, and scientific publication support services for the Aeronautics Research Mission Directorate. The applicable NAICS code is 711510 – Independent Artists, Writers, and Performers, with a size standard of $9.5 million in average annual receipts. Prospective contractors must be certified as Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business concerns through the SBA or an SBA-approved third-party certifier, and such certification must be in active standing at the time the offer is submitted and at the time of award. The Government will verify EDWOSB certification status through official SBA records. Offers submitted by firms that do not meet the EDWOSB eligibility criteria will be considered nonresponsive and excluded from evaluation. The anticipated contract type is a time-and-materials contract with a one-year base period and two one-year option periods. Offerors are advised that the contracting officer has authority to cancel this set-aside and reissue the solicitation under a different socioeconomic program or as an unrestricted procurement if adequate EDWOSB competition is not achieved.
edwosb_set_aside

Example 1:
The Department of State, Bureau of Overseas Buildings Operations, requires construction management and quality assurance services for the New Embassy Compound project in Nairobi, Kenya. All contractor construction management, inspection, and quality assurance personnel shall perform their duties at the embassy construction site located in Nairobi, Kenya. On-site presence is mandatory for all individuals performing inspection, materials testing, contractor oversight, and project documentation functions. The State Department's security and diplomatic facility standards require that all construction oversight activities be conducted in person at the Nairobi project site to ensure immediate identification and resolution of construction deficiencies. Contractor staff must sign in at the Diplomatic Security-controlled site entry point at the commencement of each work day. All field inspection reports, non-conformance notices, and quality control records must be generated and signed at the project site. The contractor shall maintain a full-time resident project manager and a qualified inspector at the Nairobi site for the duration of the construction phase. Personnel rotations must be pre-approved by the Contracting Officer and shall not result in any gap in on-site coverage.

Example 2:
The Federal Bureau of Prisons, Federal Correctional Complex Tucson, is soliciting for medical services support including physician, nursing, and mental health staffing at the FCC Tucson facility. All services shall be delivered exclusively on-site at Federal Correctional Complex Tucson, 8901 S Wilmot Rd, Tucson, AZ 85756. Given the inherent security requirements of a federal correctional environment, no contractor healthcare provider may perform any patient care, recordkeeping, or clinical consultation activities from a location outside the secured perimeter of the institution. Providers must report to the Health Services Unit at the start of each scheduled shift and must remain within the facility compound for the duration of their tour of duty. Telemedicine services are specifically excluded from this contract scope. Credentialing, scheduling, and supervision of all contractor medical personnel shall be conducted by the Health Services Administrator located at the FCC Tucson campus. Contractors must demonstrate their ability to staff all required positions with individuals who can physically report to the Tucson facility for every scheduled shift. Failure to maintain required on-site staffing levels may result in deductions under the contract's quality assurance provisions.

Example 3:
The Department of Homeland Security, U.S. Customs and Border Protection, Office of Facilities and Asset Management, requires comprehensive building operations and maintenance services for the CBP Land Port of Entry at Laredo, Texas. All contractor work shall be performed at the World Trade Bridge Port of Entry, 6002 Milo Rodriguez Jr. Blvd, Laredo, TX 78045. The physical and operational security demands of this mission-critical federal facility require that all personnel engaged under this contract be present at the port of entry during all hours of contract performance. No work may be conducted from off-site locations. Personnel must complete CBP-specific security training and receive a facility access badge before performing any duties. The contractor shall maintain a dedicated on-site supervisor during all operational shifts. Maintenance logs, inspection records, and service reports shall be completed on-site and submitted to the COR through the government's facility management system, which is accessible only from terminals located within the port of entry. Contractors found to be conducting contract-related work from unauthorized locations will be subject to immediate removal from the program.

---

RFP chunk to analyze:
STATEMENT OF OBJECTIVES – ATTACHMENT A

A.3 Operational Context and Work Environment

The program office wishes to provide offerors with accurate context regarding work environment expectations to facilitate realistic proposal development. Some legacy task orders under predecessor contracts for this program required contractor personnel to maintain a dedicated presence at the program office located in the National Capital Region. That requirement was driven by classified system access limitations that have since been resolved through the deployment of approved remote access infrastructure. As a result, the current solicitation imposes no obligation for contractor personnel to report to or maintain a presence at any specific Government facility. Contractors operating from their home offices, corporate facilities, or co-working spaces consistent with applicable security requirements are fully compliant with the place-of-performance expectations of this contract. The Government acknowledges the workforce and cost efficiencies associated with remote and hybrid arrangements and has incorporated this flexibility intentionally into the acquisition strategy. Any on-site activity that does occur—such as initial program orientation, periodic all-hands meetings, or hardware deployment support—will be planned collaboratively and will not represent a sustained or mandatory performance condition. Offerors should price their proposals accordingly, without budgeting for daily commuting or local lodging costs, as such expenses would not be reimbursable under the terms of this contract.
no_flag

Example 1:
U.S. ARMY CORPS OF ENGINEERS
SOLICITATION NO.: W912DY-24-R-0055
SET-ASIDE TYPE: EDWOSB

The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Louisville District, is soliciting offers under a total Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business (EDWOSB) set-aside for architectural and engineering services related to the design and rehabilitation of flood control infrastructure in the Ohio River watershed. This procurement is conducted in accordance with the Brooks Act (40 U.S.C. 1101 et seq.) and the Small Business Act's EDWOSB provisions at 15 U.S.C. 637(m). The NAICS code applicable to this requirement is 541330 – Engineering Services, with a size standard of $25.5 million. Only EDWOSB-certified firms may submit qualifications packages in response to this announcement. To qualify, the concern must be at least 51 percent unconditionally and directly owned and controlled by one or more women who are economically disadvantaged as defined by 13 CFR 127.200. Firms must demonstrate active EDWOSB certification through the SBA's certification platform (certify.sba.gov) or an approved third-party certifier. Failure to maintain EDWOSB certification through the date of award shall result in the offeror being deemed ineligible. Subcontracting to non-EDWOSB firms is permitted in accordance with applicable FAR provisions, provided the EDWOSB prime contractor performs the required percentage of work.

Example 2:
DEPARTMENT OF THE INTERIOR
BUREAU OF LAND MANAGEMENT
Solicitation Number: L24PX02094

The Bureau of Land Management (BLM), National Operations Center, is conducting this competitive acquisition as a total set-aside for Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business (EDWOSB) concerns pursuant to the authority granted under the Small Business Act, 15 U.S.C. 637(m), and FAR Subpart 19.15. The BLM requires geospatial information systems (GIS) mapping and cartographic services to support public lands survey and resource management planning activities across BLM-administered lands in eleven western states. NAICS code 541370 – Surveying and Mapping (except Geophysical) Services applies, with a size standard of $19 million. In accordance with FAR 52.219-30, Notice of Set-Aside for, or Sole Source Award to, Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business Concerns, only EDWOSB-certified firms are eligible to receive an award under this solicitation. To be eligible, the offeror must represent that it is an EDWOSB concern meeting all requirements of 13 CFR Part 127 and must provide documentation confirming current SBA certification or third-party certifier approval. The contracting officer will not consider offers from firms that are certified only as Women-Owned Small Businesses (WOSB) without the economically disadvantaged designation. Offerors are encouraged to review the eligibility criteria at certify.sba.gov prior to submitting a proposal.

Example 3:
SOLICITATION NUMBER: HHS-2024-EDWOSB-0042

This acquisition is a 100% set-aside for Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business (EDWOSB) concerns under the authority of 15 U.S.C. 637(m). The Department of Health and Human Services, Office of Acquisition Services, intends to award a firm-fixed-price contract for the provision of professional health information technology consulting services in support of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services modernization initiative. Only offerors who are certified Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business concerns at the time of offer submission and at the time of award are eligible to receive this contract. Offerors must be registered in the System for Award Management (SAM.gov) and must have a current EDWOSB certification through the SBA certification program or a third-party certifier approved by the Small Business Administration. The applicable NAICS code is 541512 with a small business size standard of $34 million in average annual receipts. Any offer received from a concern that does not qualify as an EDWOSB at the time of offer will be considered nonresponsive and will not be evaluated. Questions regarding EDWOSB eligibility requirements should be directed to the contracting officer prior to the closing date of this solicitation.

---

RFP chunk to analyze:
NATIONAL AERONAUTICS AND SPACE ADMINISTRATION
GODDARD SPACE FLIGHT CENTER
Solicitation Number: NNX-GSFC-2024-SA-0047

SECTION B – SUPPLIES OR SERVICES AND PRICE/COST

B.1 CONTRACT TYPE AND SET-ASIDE DESIGNATION

This solicitation is issued as a 100% Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business (EDWOSB) set-aside in accordance with FAR 19.1505(b) and the SBA regulations at 13 CFR Part 127. NASA Goddard Space Flight Center intends to award a cost-plus-fixed-fee contract for scientific data management and visualization software development services in direct support of the Earth Observing System Data and Information System (EOSDIS). The relevant NAICS code is 541511 – Custom Computer Programming Services, with an applicable small business size standard of $34 million. Eligibility for award is strictly limited to offerors that are certified Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Businesses at the time offers are submitted. Certification must have been granted by the Small Business Administration through the SBA's EDWOSB certification portal or through an SBA-recognized third-party certifying organization, and must remain active throughout the entire source selection process and at the time of contract award. Offerors are advised that self-certification alone is not sufficient to establish EDWOSB eligibility under this solicitation; official SBA certification documentation must be included with each submitted offer. NASA reserves the right to verify EDWOSB status through SAM.gov and direct inquiry with the SBA. Any offeror that cannot demonstrate current EDWOSB certification will be deemed ineligible and excluded from further consideration without further notice.
edwosb_set_aside

Example 1:
DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION
FEDERAL HIGHWAY ADMINISTRATION
SOLICITATION NUMBER: DTFH6124R00031

SET-ASIDE: Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business (SDVOSB)

The Federal Highway Administration (FHWA), Office of Infrastructure, is issuing this Request for Proposals as a total SDVOSB set-aside in accordance with FAR Subpart 19.14. This solicitation covers environmental compliance monitoring and reporting services along designated federal highway corridors in the Rocky Mountain Region. NAICS code 541620 (Environmental Consulting Services) applies with a size standard of $19 million. Only firms that qualify as Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Businesses under FAR 2.101 and 13 CFR Part 128 may submit proposals. The contracting officer has reviewed available market sources and determined that at least two or more SDVOSB concerns are capable of performing this work at competitive prices. Offerors shall provide documentation confirming SDVOSB status, including copies of relevant disability ratings and ownership certifications, with their technical proposal submissions. FHWA reserves the right to cancel the SDVOSB set-aside designation and re-solicit under a different socioeconomic category should adequate competition not materialize.

Example 2:
DEPARTMENT OF VETERANS AFFAIRS
VETERANS HEALTH ADMINISTRATION

The Department of Housing and Urban Development issues this solicitation for data analytics platform services and requires, as part of the competitive evaluation, that offerors participate in a structured design challenge demonstrating their platform's analytical capabilities. The design challenge will provide each offeror with a HUD-supplied housing market dataset containing deliberately introduced data quality issues, missing records, and anomalous entries. Offeror teams will have five (5) hours to cleanse the dataset, perform trend analysis, develop predictive model outputs, and present findings in a final briefing to HUD evaluators. The design challenge is intended to replicate conditions representative of actual task order performance and will be evaluated on analytical rigor, interpretive accuracy, visualization quality, and presentation clarity. No more than five (5) offeror personnel may participate in the design challenge. HUD will provide workstations loaded with a baseline software suite; offerors wishing to use additional licensed tools must request approval from the Contracting Officer no fewer than seven (7) days before the scheduled exercise date. Results will directly inform the Technical Capability and Staffing evaluation factors as described in Section M.

Example 2:
The Department of Transportation invites qualified vendors to participate in a mandatory design challenge as part of this solicitation process. Offerors shall develop and submit a working prototype of their proposed traffic management interface within thirty (30) calendar days of solicitation release. The prototype must demonstrate core functionality including real-time data ingestion, alert escalation workflows, and dashboard visualization. Government evaluators will conduct hands-on testing of each submitted prototype during a structured demonstration session scheduled at DOT headquarters in Washington, D.C. Scoring criteria for the design challenge component account for forty percent (40%) of the overall technical evaluation score. Prototypes that fail to meet minimum functionality thresholds as defined in Attachment C will be deemed technically unacceptable and removed from further consideration. Offerors are encouraged to review the Technical Evaluation Criteria in Section M prior to initiating prototype development. All prototype submissions must be delivered via secure government portal no later than 5:00 PM Eastern Time on the date specified in the solicitation timeline.

Example 3:
The General Services Administration hereby issues this solicitation which includes a required design exercise component for all competitive range offerors. Specifically, each offeror must submit a prototype submission demonstrating their proposed citizen-facing web portal solution. The prototype submission shall be a fully navigable, hosted demonstration environment accessible via government-provided credentials. The prototype must include functional user registration, document upload, and status tracking modules. GSA evaluators will engage directly with the prototype during a structured two-hour evaluation session and will simulate end-user workflows as described in Attachment D, User Scenario Matrix. Prototype submissions are due no later than fourteen (14) days prior to the scheduled oral presentations. Offerors are advised that the prototype submission will be evaluated independently from the written technical volume and carries a weight of thirty-five percent (35%) of the total technical score. Any prototype that is unavailable, incomplete, or non-functional during the evaluation session will receive a rating of Unacceptable for that factor.

The Department of Veterans Affairs is seeking a Commercial Off-the-Shelf (COTS) solution for enterprise human resources management. Offerors must provide a fully licensed, commercially available software platform that meets the functional requirements outlined in this solicitation. No custom software development will be performed under this contract. The Government requires configuration only to align the solution with VA-specific workflows, user roles, and reporting hierarchies. Vendors who propose proprietary or purpose-built applications developed specifically for this requirement will be considered non-responsive. The selected solution must be a product currently available in the commercial marketplace with an established user base of no fewer than fifty enterprise customers. Implementation services shall be limited to system configuration, data migration, and user training. All modifications to core application functionality are expressly prohibited. The Contractor shall document all configuration settings to ensure repeatability and auditability. Any identified gaps between COTS functionality and agency requirements shall be addressed through process re-engineering on the Government side, not through development of new software features.

Example 2:
Pursuant to Federal Acquisition Regulation Part 12, the Department of Education is conducting a commercial item acquisition for a learning management system to support Title I program administration training. This solicitation does not contemplate and will not fund any new software engineering effort. No custom development of application features, modules, dashboards, or reporting tools is included within scope. The awarded Contractor shall be responsible for configuration only, including course catalog setup, learner enrollment workflows, administrator role assignments, and integration with the Department's single sign-on environment using the platform's existing API connectors. The Government acknowledges that the selected COTS platform may not fulfill one hundred percent of desired capabilities and accepts that business processes will be adapted accordingly. Offerors should not propose feature enhancements or product roadmap commitments as part of their technical approach. Evaluation criteria will focus on the extent to which the existing, unmodified platform addresses stated requirements without reliance on future development promises.

Example 3:
The Department of Homeland Security, Office of the Chief Information Officer, hereby solicits proposals for an enterprise identity and access management solution. This requirement is for a COTS solution required to meet the agency's zero-trust architecture objectives. The Government will not fund, authorize, or accept delivery of any custom-developed software components. Offerors are advised that the scope of this engagement is configuration only, encompassing identity provider setup, multi-factor authentication policy definition, role-based access control mapping, and directory synchronization. All proposed software must be commercially available and actively maintained by the Offeror as a standard product offering. Licensing terms must reflect commercial market rates, and the product must receive regular security patches and version updates as part of the vendor's standard release cycle. Integration connectors used to link the platform with DHS legacy systems must be pre-built and commercially supported. Engineering of new connectors or middleware specifically for this contract is outside the permissible scope of work.

The contractor shall be responsible for end-to-end delivery of software capabilities, including custom application development, systems integration, automated testing frameworks, and continuous delivery pipeline management. Offerors should carefully review Section C of this solicitation, which details the technical requirements associated with building new microservices architecture components. Some prior procurements in this portfolio involved configuration-only engagements where custom development was explicitly excluded from scope; this acquisition differs materially from those efforts and imposes affirmative software engineering obligations on the awardee. The distinction is significant for pricing and staffing purposes. Labor categories proposed must include software engineers at multiple grade levels, and the technical volume shall address the offeror's software development methodology in detail. The Government anticipates issuing multiple task orders under the resulting Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity contract, with individual task orders specifying the precise mix of development, integration, and sustainment activities required for each program increment.

The contractor shall provide all personnel necessary to perform the scope of work described herein. All work under this contract shall be performed onsite at the Department of Agriculture's regional administrative offices located at 8030 Excelsior Drive, Madison, Wisconsin 53717. Remote work arrangements will not be authorized under any circumstances. Contractor employees must report to the Madison, WI facility no later than 7:30 AM on each scheduled workday. The Government will provide workspace, network access terminals, and badged entry to the facility. Contractors must comply with all Federal building security protocols, including identity verification and visitor management procedures enforced at the Madison location. Travel reimbursement to and from the Madison facility on a daily basis is not an allowable cost under this contract. The offeror shall demonstrate in their technical approach a plan for staffing qualified personnel within commutable distance of Madison, Wisconsin, or provide documentation of relocation plans for proposed key personnel prior to contract award. All deliverables, including reports, data analyses, and briefing materials, shall be completed and submitted from the Madison, WI performance site.

Example 2:
The Social Security Administration, Great Lakes Program Service Center, hereby solicits proposals for records management and document digitization services. This is a full-time onsite requirement; all contractor employees must perform work at the SSA facility in Madison, Wisconsin. The Madison, WI site contains sensitive personally identifiable information that may not be removed from the premises under any conditions. As a result, contractor personnel must complete all assigned tasks within the secure boundaries of the Madison facility. Proposals that include provisions for off-site processing or remote access to document systems will be deemed technically unacceptable. The Government estimates a workforce of ten to fifteen contractor full-time equivalents performing onsite in Madison for the base year. Offerors shall include a staffing plan that explicitly addresses local recruitment within the Madison, Wisconsin labor market and identifies any planned subcontracting arrangements with firms based in the Madison area.

Example 3:
The Department of Transportation's Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration issues this solicitation for compliance audit and enforcement support services. Performance shall occur onsite at FMCSA's Madison, Wisconsin district office. All audit personnel must be physically present at the Madison, WI office when conducting case reviews, preparing enforcement documents, and attending agency coordination meetings. FMCSA does not permit remote access to enforcement case management systems from locations outside of the Madison facility. The contractor shall maintain a minimum of four full-time audit specialists onsite in Madison at all times during the contract period of performance. Proposed personnel must provide documentation confirming their ability to commute to or reside in Madison, Wisconsin. The contractor's quality control plan shall describe procedures for monitoring onsite attendance and performance at the Madison location. Unexcused absences from the Madison, WI facility may constitute grounds for a cure notice under the terms of this contract.

RFP chunk to analyze:
The Department of Homeland Security, Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), is soliciting proposals for custom software development services to build an automated threat intelligence aggregation and dissemination platform. The system shall collect structured and unstructured threat data from multiple government and open-source feeds, normalize the data into STIX/TAXII-compliant formats, and provide automated distribution to registered partner organizations across critical infrastructure sectors. The contractor shall develop a web-based administrative interface for CISA analysts to manage data sources, apply filtering rules, and review dissemination logs. The platform shall be architected to accommodate future integration with additional intelligence sharing frameworks and shall include comprehensive audit logging to support oversight requirements. The government requires the contractor to maintain a dedicated security engineering resource throughout development to conduct continuous threat modeling and remediation of identified vulnerabilities. The contract type is time-and-materials with a not-to-exceed ceiling for the base year and each option year. Offerors shall submit a written technical approach of no more than 25 pages, a staffing plan, and a detailed price proposal. Evaluation criteria include technical approach, key personnel qualifications, past performance on cybersecurity software development efforts, and total evaluated price.

Example 1:
DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION
FEDERAL HIGHWAY ADMINISTRATION
Solicitation Number: FHWA-2024-SS-00091

NOTICE OF SET-ASIDE: The Federal Highway Administration (FHWA), in accordance with the Women-Owned Small Business Federal Contract Program established under 15 U.S.C. 637(m) and implemented at FAR Subpart 19.15, hereby designates this procurement as a total set-aside for Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business (EDWOSB) concerns. The FHWA requires professional environmental consulting services to support National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) compliance reviews for proposed highway infrastructure projects in the Mountain West region. The applicable NAICS code is 541620 – Environmental Consulting Services, with a size standard of $19 million in average annual receipts. The contracting officer's market research has determined that there is a reasonable expectation that two or more EDWOSB concerns capable of performing the required work at a fair market price will submit offers. Eligible offerors must be certified as Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Businesses through the SBA's EDWOSB certification process. Firms that are certified only as Women-Owned Small Businesses (WOSB) but not as Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Businesses are not eligible for this set-aside. All EDWOSB certifications must be current and reflected in SAM.gov at time of offer.

Example 2:
DEPARTMENT OF THE INTERIOR
BUREAU OF LAND MANAGEMENT
Solicitation Number: L24PX02094

The Bureau of Land Management (BLM), National Operations Center, is conducting this competitive acquisition as a total set-aside for Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business (EDWOSB) concerns pursuant to the authority granted under the Small Business Act, 15 U.S.C. 637(m), and FAR Subpart 19.15. The BLM requires geospatial information systems (GIS) mapping and cartographic services to support public lands survey and resource management planning activities across BLM-administered lands in eleven western states. NAICS code 541370 – Surveying and Mapping (except Geophysical) Services applies, with a size standard of $19 million. In accordance with FAR 52.219-30, Notice of Set-Aside for, or Sole Source Award to, Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business Concerns, only EDWOSB-certified firms are eligible to receive an award under this solicitation. To be eligible, the offeror must represent that it is an EDWOSB concern meeting all requirements of 13 CFR Part 127 and must provide documentation confirming current SBA certification or third-party certifier approval. The contracting officer will not consider offers from firms that are certified only as Women-Owned Small Businesses (WOSB) without the economically disadvantaged designation. Offerors are encouraged to review the eligibility criteria at certify.sba.gov prior to submitting a proposal.

Example 3:
U.S. ARMY CORPS OF ENGINEERS
SOLICITATION NO.: W912DY-24-R-0055
SET-ASIDE TYPE: EDWOSB

The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Louisville District, is soliciting offers under a total Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business (EDWOSB) set-aside for architectural and engineering services related to the design and rehabilitation of flood control infrastructure in the Ohio River watershed. This procurement is conducted in accordance with the Brooks Act (40 U.S.C. 1101 et seq.) and the Small Business Act's EDWOSB provisions at 15 U.S.C. 637(m). The NAICS code applicable to this requirement is 541330 – Engineering Services, with a size standard of $25.5 million. Only EDWOSB-certified firms may submit qualifications packages in response to this announcement. To qualify, the concern must be at least 51 percent unconditionally and directly owned and controlled by one or more women who are economically disadvantaged as defined by 13 CFR 127.200. Firms must demonstrate active EDWOSB certification through the SBA's certification platform (certify.sba.gov) or an approved third-party certifier. Failure to maintain EDWOSB certification through the date of award shall result in the offeror being deemed ineligible. Subcontracting to non-EDWOSB firms is permitted in accordance with applicable FAR provisions, provided the EDWOSB prime contractor performs the required percentage of work.

RFP chunk to analyze:
The General Services Administration (GSA), Federal Acquisition Service, Technology Transformation Services, is soliciting proposals for the development and deployment of a Digital Identity Verification and Credential Management Service to support multiple federal agency authentication use cases. This acquisition is structured as an Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity contract with a five-year ordering period and a maximum ceiling value of forty million dollars. Individual task orders will be issued on a competitive basis among awardees for discrete capability increments, pilot programs, and production deployments. The base technical requirement encompasses design and development of identity proofing workflows compliant with NIST Special Publication 800-63-3 at Identity Assurance Level 2, integration modules for agency relying party connections via OpenID Connect and SAML 2.0 protocols, an administrative console for agency configuration management, and instrumentation supporting audit logging and anomaly detection. The contractor shall work collaboratively with GSA product teams and agency partners to conduct user research, develop and test prototypes, and refine system behavior based on empirical usability and security findings. All software artifacts shall be licensed to the Government under an unlimited rights license. Source selection will be conducted on a best value basis with separate technical and price evaluation factors; technical factors are significantly more important than price in the overall source selection determination. The applicable NAICS code is 541519 and the size standard is $34 million.

This solicitation for Healthcare Information Technology Modernization Support Services includes an oral presentation requirement as a formal element of the evaluation process. The Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) will invite offerors in the competitive range to participate in structured oral presentations following the initial written proposal review. Oral presentations will be assessed under Technical Factor 2 – Implementation Methodology and Technical Factor 4 – Relevant Experience. Each offeror will have seventy-five (75) minutes to present, followed by a twenty-five (25) minute question-and-answer session conducted by the HHS Evaluation Committee. The offeror's proposed Program Manager, Lead Systems Architect, and at least one clinical informatics specialist must be present in person. Remote participation is not authorized except under emergency circumstances approved in advance by the Contracting Officer. Offerors should structure their oral presentation to directly address the evaluation criteria outlined in Section M and are advised that new information introduced during the oral presentation that was not contained in the written proposal will be evaluated as a clarification and not as a revision or amendment to the offer.

Example 2:
The Government reserves the right to conduct oral presentations with offerors in the competitive range. Oral presentations will be scheduled following the initial evaluation of written proposals. Each offeror shall be prepared to present and discuss their technical approach, management strategy, and key personnel qualifications before a panel of Government evaluators. The oral presentation shall not exceed ninety (90) minutes, inclusive of a question-and-answer period not to exceed thirty (30) minutes. Offerors may use visual aids such as slides or diagrams; however, all materials presented during the oral presentation must be submitted in writing to the Contracting Officer no later than 48 hours prior to the scheduled session. Information disclosed during the oral presentation will be considered part of the offeror's proposal and will be evaluated under the Technical Approach and Key Personnel factors. The Government will not compensate offerors for costs associated with preparation or participation in oral presentations. Specific scheduling instructions will be provided to offerors selected for the competitive range via written notification from the Contracting Officer.

Example 3:
Proposals submitted in response to this solicitation will undergo a multi-phase evaluation process. Phase I consists of written technical and price proposal review. Offerors achieving a combined written evaluation score that places them within the competitive range will advance to Phase II, which requires an oral presentation before the Department of Transportation Technical Review Board. The oral presentation phase is designed to supplement written proposals by allowing offerors to demonstrate technical depth, address evaluator questions regarding proposed approaches, and introduce key personnel who will execute the contract. Oral presentations will be evaluated under Factor 1 – Technical Approach and Factor 3 – Key Personnel, and scores derived from the oral presentation will be weighted equally with written proposal scores for those factors. Each presentation is allocated ninety (90) minutes, with no more than sixty (60) minutes reserved for the offeror's presentation and the remainder designated for Government questions. The offeror's Project Manager and at least two senior technical staff members named in the proposal must be physically present.

The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Southwestern Division, hereby issues this solicitation for SharePoint-based knowledge management system services. The Division has standardized on Microsoft SharePoint Online as its enterprise collaboration platform under the Army's existing M365 enterprise agreement and requires a contractor to configure and deploy a structured knowledge repository supporting engineering project documentation, lessons learned capture, and cross-district information sharing. The contractor shall design and implement a SharePoint site collection hierarchy, configure content types, managed metadata term stores, search schema, and retention policies consistent with Army Records Management requirements. The Government does not require and will not fund proprietary software development under this task order. The contractor shall provide all configuration through SharePoint's native administrative interfaces and approved Power Platform tools. Training shall be provided for up to 60 knowledge managers across six district offices. Final deliverables include a fully operational configured SharePoint environment and comprehensive administrator and end-user documentation.

Example 2:
The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Science and Technology Directorate is soliciting proposals for the implementation of a cloud-based project portfolio management capability to support research program tracking across the Directorate. Following a formal analysis of alternatives, the Government has selected a commercial solution based on Salesforce Project Management modules as the technical baseline. The primary work under this contract is configuring and deploying the selected commercial off-the-shelf platform to meet DHS-specific reporting, resource allocation, and milestone tracking requirements. The contractor shall configure dashboards, automated notifications, role-based access controls, and integration connectors to existing financial management systems. Offerors shall describe their approach to Salesforce configuration without reliance on custom Apex code development except where configuration-only approaches are technically infeasible and documented as such. The Government anticipates a total level of effort of approximately 2,800 hours across the base year, with options for ongoing platform administration and enhancement.

Example 3:
The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) Office of Education invites qualified vendors to submit proposals for the redesign and deployment of a public educational outreach website. NOAA has determined that a pre-built platform such as Squarespace or an equivalent commercially available website builder satisfies the functional needs of this effort, which is primarily informational in nature and does not require custom application development or backend database integration. The contractor shall configure and deploy the selected commercial platform, ensuring full compliance with NOAA digital branding standards, Section 508 accessibility requirements, and applicable federal web policy. Deliverables include site architecture documentation, configured and populated site with a minimum of 50 content pages, staff training for designated content editors, and a 12-month support retainer for platform administration. Proposals shall identify the specific commercial off-the-shelf platform proposed, justify its selection against the functional requirements in Section C, and provide a fixed-price implementation schedule.

The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) Office of Education and Outreach hereby issues this Request for Proposals for the redesign and deployment of its public science communication web presence. NOAA has determined through market research that a commercial off-the-shelf content management solution represents the most appropriate and cost-effective approach to meet the agency's digital communications requirements. The Government has selected Drupal as the foundational platform and intends to leverage the existing open-source commercial solution rather than commission custom software development. The contractor shall configure and deploy a Drupal-based website architecture consistent with the U.S. Web Design System standards, OMB M-23-22 digital experience guidance, and Section 508 accessibility requirements. Scope of work includes information architecture development, theme configuration, module selection and hardening, multilingual content support configuration, and integration with NOAA's enterprise authentication services. The contractor shall populate the site with approximately 1,800 content items migrated from the legacy Joomla environment and shall conduct accessibility compliance testing prior to go-live. Custom application development is expressly excluded from this procurement. Post-launch support shall include a twelve-month maintenance period covering security patching, module updates, and content management system administrator support. Proposals must include a detailed configuration and deployment methodology, a migration approach narrative, and at least two past performance references demonstrating comparable Drupal deployments for federal science or education programs.

Huntsville Center

SMALL BUSINESS PROGRAM: HUBZone Set-Aside

This procurement is conducted as a competitive HUBZone small business set-aside pursuant to 15 U.S.C. § 657a and FAR 19.1305(a). Public Buildings Service
Request for Proposals — Janitorial and Custodial Services
Solicitation: 47PA0124R0012

Award is anticipated to be a single firm-fixed-price indefinite delivery/indefinite quantity contract.

RFP chunk to analyze:
The Department of Veterans Affairs, Office of Information and Technology, hereby solicits proposals for the design, development, and deployment of a cloud-native case management platform to support the Benefits Coordination Division. The Government requires a contractor to develop a custom software solution capable of integrating with existing VA enterprise systems, including but not limited to the Corporate Database and the Veterans Benefits Management System, via secure RESTful APIs. The platform shall provide end-to-end tracking of veteran benefit claims, incorporating role-based access controls, audit logging, and real-time status notifications delivered via web and mobile interfaces. The contractor shall conduct discovery and requirements refinement activities in close collaboration with VA product owners and subject matter experts during the initial phase of performance. The solution must be built in accordance with VA Technical Reference Model standards and shall be hosted within a FedRAMP-authorized cloud environment at the Moderate impact level. The contractor shall provide full documentation, including system architecture diagrams, data flow diagrams, user guides, and an authority to operate package in accordance with NIST 800-53 revision 5 controls. The period of performance consists of a twelve-month base period with two twelve-month option periods. The contract vehicle is a firm-fixed-price task order under the agency's existing Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity contract. Source selection will be conducted using a best value tradeoff approach considering technical approach, relevant past performance, and price as evaluation factors, with technical factors weighted more heavily than price.

The Environmental Protection Agency, Office of Environmental Information, requires a hands-on exercise as a component of the technical evaluation for this Blanket Purchase Agreement competition. Each offeror will be given access to a standardized environmental monitoring dataset and tasked with producing a data quality analysis report and visualization dashboard within a three-hour period. The hands-on exercise will be conducted at EPA's Research Triangle Park facility. Offerors must bring all necessary hardware and licensed software; EPA will provide only network connectivity and access to the standardized dataset via a secure file transfer mechanism. Evaluators will assess the quality, accuracy, and clarity of the analysis report and dashboard outputs using criteria specified in Section M.4. The hands-on exercise score will be combined with written technical proposal scores to produce a composite technical rating. Offerors are advised that strong written proposals will not compensate for poor performance on the hands-on exercise, as the Agency views demonstrated capability as equally important to proposed methodology. All outputs produced during the exercise must be submitted to the Contracting Officer's Representative before participants depart the facility.

Example 2:
The Department of Transportation invites qualified vendors to participate in a mandatory design challenge as part of this solicitation process. Offerors shall develop and submit a working prototype of their proposed traffic management interface within thirty (30) calendar days of solicitation release. The prototype must demonstrate core functionality including real-time data ingestion, alert escalation workflows, and dashboard visualization. Government evaluators will conduct hands-on testing of each submitted prototype during a structured demonstration session scheduled at DOT headquarters in Washington, D.C. Scoring criteria for the design challenge component account for forty percent (40%) of the overall technical evaluation score. Prototypes that fail to meet minimum functionality thresholds as defined in Attachment C will be deemed technically unacceptable and removed from further consideration. Offerors are encouraged to review the Technical Evaluation Criteria in Section M prior to initiating prototype development. All prototype submissions must be delivered via secure government portal no later than 5:00 PM Eastern Time on the date specified in the solicitation timeline.

Example 3:
The General Services Administration hereby issues this solicitation which includes a required design exercise component for all competitive range offerors. Specifically, each offeror must submit a prototype submission demonstrating their proposed citizen-facing web portal solution. The prototype submission shall be a fully navigable, hosted demonstration environment accessible via government-provided credentials. The prototype must include functional user registration, document upload, and status tracking modules. GSA evaluators will engage directly with the prototype during a structured two-hour evaluation session and will simulate end-user workflows as described in Attachment D, User Scenario Matrix. Prototype submissions are due no later than fourteen (14) days prior to the scheduled oral presentations. Offerors are advised that the prototype submission will be evaluated independently from the written technical volume and carries a weight of thirty-five percent (35%) of the total technical score. Any prototype that is unavailable, incomplete, or non-functional during the evaluation session will receive a rating of Unacceptable for that factor.

Prototypes that are inaccessible, crash during testing, or fail to complete more than fifty percent (50%) of the test scenarios will receive an Unacceptable rating for this evaluation element, rendering the overall proposal ineligible for award.

Example 1:
Proposals submitted in response to this solicitation will undergo a multi-phase evaluation process. Phase I consists of written technical and price proposal review. Offerors achieving a combined written evaluation score that places them within the competitive range will advance to Phase II, which requires an oral presentation before the Department of Transportation Technical Review Board. The oral presentation phase is designed to supplement written proposals by allowing offerors to demonstrate technical depth, address evaluator questions regarding proposed approaches, and introduce key personnel who will execute the contract. Oral presentations will be evaluated under Factor 1 – Technical Approach and Factor 3 – Key Personnel, and scores derived from the oral presentation will be weighted equally with written proposal scores for those factors. Each presentation is allocated ninety (90) minutes, with no more than sixty (60) minutes reserved for the offeror's presentation and the remainder designated for Government questions. The offeror's Project Manager and at least two senior technical staff members named in the proposal must be physically present.

Example 2:
The Government reserves the right to conduct oral presentations with offerors in the competitive range. Oral presentations will be scheduled following the initial evaluation of written proposals. Each offeror shall be prepared to present and discuss their technical approach, management strategy, and key personnel qualifications before a panel of Government evaluators. The oral presentation shall not exceed ninety (90) minutes, inclusive of a question-and-answer period not to exceed thirty (30) minutes. Offerors may use visual aids such as slides or diagrams; however, all materials presented during the oral presentation must be submitted in writing to the Contracting Officer no later than 48 hours prior to the scheduled session. Information disclosed during the oral presentation will be considered part of the offeror's proposal and will be evaluated under the Technical Approach and Key Personnel factors. The Government will not compensate offerors for costs associated with preparation or participation in oral presentations. Specific scheduling instructions will be provided to offerors selected for the competitive range via written notification from the Contracting Officer.

Example 3:
In accordance with FAR 15.102, this solicitation incorporates oral presentations as a substitute for, or supplement to, written information for the Technical Approach evaluation factor. Offerors selected for oral presentations will receive written notification from the Contracting Officer specifying the date, time, location, and format requirements. The Department of Energy's Office of Environmental Management will evaluate the oral presentation under Subfactor 1A – Technical Understanding and Subfactor 1B – Staffing and Organization. Each offeror's presentation team must include the proposed Project Director, Deputy Project Director, and at least one subject matter expert for each major technical area described in the Statement of Work. Presentations shall not exceed one hundred and fifty (150) minutes inclusive of Government questions. Offerors are encouraged to use visual aids, technical demonstrations, or mock deliverable examples to enhance their presentations. The evaluation panel will score the oral presentation independently from written submissions, and both scores will be combined to produce the final Technical Approach rating used in the Best Value determination.

L.14 Oral Presentation Requirements. As part of the evaluation process for this Information Technology Modernization Services contract, the General Services Administration (GSA) intends to conduct oral presentations with offerors determined to be in the competitive range following the initial review of written submissions. The purpose of the oral presentation is to assess the offeror's depth of understanding of GSA's mission requirements, the feasibility of the proposed technical solution, and the qualifications of proposed key personnel. Each oral presentation shall be scheduled for no more than ninety (90) minutes, inclusive of a mandatory question-and-answer period of no less than twenty (20) minutes. GSA evaluators will score the oral presentation using the same evaluation criteria applied to the written technical volume, specifically under Factor 1 – Technical Approach and Factor 3 – Key Personnel. Offerors are advised that any representations made verbally during the oral presentation that are inconsistent with the written proposal must be reconciled in writing within forty-eight (48) hours of the session. GSA reserves the right to record oral presentation sessions for internal evaluation purposes. Offerors will receive written notification of their scheduled date and time no fewer than seven (7) calendar days in advance. The Government will not reimburse offerors for any costs incurred in connection with preparation for or participation in the oral presentation.

This acquisition will be evaluated using the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection process in accordance with FAR 15.101-2. Technical acceptability will be evaluated under three pass/fail criteria: Key Personnel Qualifications, Laboratory Accreditation Compliance, and Quality Assurance Plan Adequacy. Proposals must be rated Acceptable on all three criteria to be eligible for award. Technical proposals that exceed minimum requirements will receive no additional consideration. Following determination of technical acceptability, award will be made to the responsible offeror submitting the lowest evaluated total price. The Government has made a written determination that LPTA is the appropriate evaluation approach given the well-defined nature of the work and the availability of qualified contractors capable of performing at minimum standards. Questions regarding the LPTA evaluation criteria must be submitted by the deadline specified in Section L.

In accordance with FAR 15.101-2, this acquisition employs the lowest price technically acceptable (LPTA) evaluation method. Offerors must certify that all proposed equipment meets or exceeds the minimum technical specifications listed in Attachment A. Proposals that do not meet all mandatory specifications will be rated Unacceptable and removed from consideration. Among technically acceptable proposals, the Government will select for award the offer representing the lowest total evaluated price. Offerors should not include options or features beyond those required, as additional capabilities will provide no competitive advantage under this LPTA process. All hardware must be TAA compliant and originate from approved sources.

Example 1:
Department of Homeland Security, Transportation Security Administration, hereby solicits offers for a limited-scope human factors usability study of proposed checkpoint signage modifications at a single mid-sized domestic airport. The resulting contract will be firm-fixed-price with a total obligated value not to exceed $69,000. This includes all labor for study design, participant recruitment coordination, data collection, analysis, and final report preparation. The TSA Office of Security Capabilities has confirmed that $69,000 represents the entirety of funding allocated for this effort under the current fiscal year research budget, and no carryover or supplemental appropriation is anticipated. The period of performance is five months from date of award. Proposals must include a full technical approach and a price proposal that does not exceed sixty-nine thousand dollars ($69,000) in total. Offerors should note that this is a hard ceiling, not an estimated cost, and any proposal with a total price above $69,000 will be removed from the competitive range and receive no further consideration.

Example 2:
The Department of Veterans Affairs, Office of Information and Technology, hereby solicits proposals for the development and deployment of a patient scheduling optimization tool for use at one regional VA medical center. The total ceiling for this contract shall not exceed $85,000 for a base period of performance of twelve (12) months. No option periods are anticipated at this time. Offerors are advised that all technical deliverables, including requirements documentation, system design, development, testing, training, and post-deployment support, must be accomplished within the stated ceiling amount. The Government does not intend to award a contract exceeding this threshold under any circumstances. This acquisition is set aside exclusively for small businesses under NAICS code 541511. Interested parties should note that the contracting officer has determined that $85,000 represents a fair and reasonable price for the described scope of work based on independent government cost estimates. Proposals that exceed this maximum budget will be deemed non-responsive and will not be evaluated further. Offerors must clearly demonstrate how all required tasks can be completed within the $85,000 total contract value.

Example 3:
The Small Business Administration, Office of Disaster Assistance, requires data entry and document processing support services for its Fort Worth, Texas processing center. This acquisition is structured as a Time-and-Materials task order to be placed against an existing SBA Indefinite Delivery contract. The total funded amount for this task order is $93,000, representing the maximum labor and other direct cost reimbursement available. The Government has budgeted no contingency above this figure. Offerors must propose labor categories and hourly rates that, when multiplied by the estimated 2,400 hours of performance, do not exceed the $93,000 ceiling inclusive of all applicable materials and ODCs. The period of performance is six months. Proposals must include a detailed staffing plan and a cost narrative demonstrating how all tasks will be completed within the ninety-three thousand dollar ($93,000) total funded value. Submissions exceeding this ceiling will be rejected without further evaluation.

RFP chunk to analyze:
The Department of Energy, Office of Science, is soliciting proposals for the development of a custom laboratory information management system (LIMS) to support high-performance computing and experimental research workflows at a national research facility. The system shall manage sample tracking, experimental metadata, instrument scheduling, and data provenance records across multiple research programs. The contractor shall conduct a structured discovery phase to document existing workflows and define detailed system requirements in collaboration with facility staff. The resulting system shall expose programmatic APIs to allow integration with existing scientific computing pipelines and data repositories maintained by the facility. The contractor is expected to deliver a fully tested, documented, and deployed production system, along with administrator and end-user documentation and a training package for facility personnel. The government will provide access to a development and staging environment for use throughout the period of performance. Work may be performed remotely with periodic travel to participate in scheduled milestone reviews. The acquisition is structured as a cost-plus-fixed-fee contract with a 15-month base period and one 12-month option period. Source selection will be conducted using a best value tradeoff process. Technical approach and past performance are the most important evaluation factors. Offerors with demonstrable experience building LIMS or scientific data management platforms for research-intensive organizations are strongly encouraged to apply.
